steps.title: Use the x-intercept shortcut
steps.content: The graph represents all combinations of M-type stars and K-type stars with total mass $127{,}882$ quettagrams. At the $x$-intercept, $y=0$, so there are no K-type stars. That means the whole mass is coming only from the M-type stars.

steps.title: Divide total mass by the intercept
steps.content: Read the $x$-intercept from the graph. It is a little less than $160$, about $158$. If $m$ is the mass of each M-type star, then $$158m\approx127{,}882$$ so $$m\approx\frac{127{,}882}{158}\approx809.4.$$ Even if you round the intercept to $160$, you get about $799.3$, which is still closest to $811$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The answer choices are spread far enough apart that the graph estimate is enough. The computed mass is closest to $811$, so choice A is correct.
